formulas.name: Law of Alternation of Generations
formulas.formula: Alternation of generations in plant life cycle: sporophyte and gametophyte
formulas.notes: This law explains the two alternating generations in the life cycle of plants, which is a characteristic feature of plants and algae.

formulas.name: Bentham and Hooker's Classification
formulas.formula: Classification of flowering plants based on number of carpels and number of cotyledons
formulas.variables: Monocotyledons (1 cotyledon) and Dicotyledons (2 cotyledons), and the number of carpels
formulas.notes: This classification system categorizes flowering plants into monocots and dicots based on the number of cotyledons and carpels.

formulas.name: Wallace's Classification
formulas.formula: Classification of flowering plants into three groups based on the number of carpels and number of stamens
formulas.variables: Monoadelphous (with a single bundle of stamens), Polyadelphous (with multiple bundles of stamens), and Syncarpous (multiple carpels united) and Apocarpous (free carpels)
formulas.notes: This classification system categorizes flowering plants into three groups based on the number of carpels and stamens.

formulas.name: Morphological Characters
formulas.formula: Distinguishing features of monocots and dicots
formulas.variables: Monocots: one cotyledon, scattered vascular tissue, and fibrous roots, Dicots: two cotyledons, vascular tissue in a ring, and tap roots
formulas.notes: This formula lists the key morphological features that distinguish monocots from dicots.

formulas.name: Anatomical Characters
formulas.formula: Distinguishing features of monocots and dicots based on xylem and phloem
formulas.variables: Monocots: collateral xylem and phloem, Dicots: bicollateral xylem and phloem
formulas.notes: This formula lists the key anatomical features that distinguish monocots from dicots based on the arrangement of xylem and phloem tissues.
